actor_claims.quote_text: Mac-Gray should continue to seek a higher value for stockholders
CSC/Pamplona submitted a proposal for an all-cash purchase price of $20.75 per share
CSC, Pamplona and Mac-Gray executed the exclusivity agreement
Pamplona was committing to fund 100% of the capital needed for closing
Party A reiterated in a telephone call to a representative of BofA Merrill Lynch
Party B also submitted a revised indication of interest
Party C did not submit a revised indication of interest
representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch participated in a telephone conference call
representatives from BofA Merrill Lynch and Goodwin Procter
Kirkland & Ellis LLP, outside legal counsel to CSC/Pamplona
whom we refer to as Kirkland
at the instruction of the Transaction Committee
On September 19, 2013, the Special Committee held an in-person meeting
Mr. MacDonald voiced his general support for continuing with the process
Mr. Shea again reviewed the Projections
Mr. Rothenberg indicated he would like to discuss the possibility of an equity rollover
Moab would not rollover any Moab shares in the transaction
Mr. Daoust reported to the Special Committee

bid_claims.quote_text: On September 18, 2013, CSC/Pamplona submitted a proposal for an all-cash purchase price of $20.75 per share

Party A reiterated in a telephone call to a representative of BofA Merrill Lynch that its previous indication of interest with an all-cash purchase price of $18.00 to $19.00 per share was its best and final offer

bid_claims.consideration_type: $19.00 of cash to be paid at closing and the remaining per share price to be paid in the form of options
bid_claims.quote_text: On September 18, 2013, Party B also submitted a revised indication of interest with a purchase price that Party B valued at $21.50 per share
Party C did not submit a revised indication of interest or reiterate its prior indication of interest of an all-cash purchase price of $16.00 to $17.00 per share
CSC/Pamplona were willing to increase their all-cash purchase price to $21.25 per share as a last and best offer
which, together with the all-cash per share purchase price of $21.25, we refer to as the CSC/Pamplona final proposal

including the $21.25 per share cash consideration, the $15 million CSC reverse termination fee, certain post-signing covenants, the fact that there would be no financing contingency and that Pamplona was committing to fund 100% of the capital needed for closing

event_claims.description: CSC/Pamplona submitted a proposal for an all-cash purchase price of $20.75 per share and requested exclusive negotiations for two weeks.
event_claims.quote_text: On September 18, 2013, CSC/Pamplona submitted a proposal for an all-cash purchase price of $20.75 per share, reiterating that Pamplona was committed to provide 100% of the capital required to consummate the transaction and requesting that CSC/Pamplona and Mac-Gray agree to engage in exclusive negotiations for two weeks

event_claims.description: Party A reiterated that its previous indication of interest was its best and final offer.
event_claims.quote_text: Also on September 18, 2013, Party A reiterated in a telephone call to a representative of BofA Merrill Lynch that its previous indication of interest with an all-cash purchase price of $18.00 to $19.00 per share was its best and final offer

event_claims.description: Party B submitted a revised indication of interest valued at $21.50 per share.
event_claims.quote_text: On September 18, 2013, Party B also submitted a revised indication of interest with a purchase price that Party B valued at $21.50 per share

event_claims.description: Party C did not submit a revised indication of interest or reiterate its prior indication of interest.
event_claims.quote_text: Party C did not submit a revised indication of interest or reiterate its prior indication of interest of an all-cash purchase price of $16.00 to $17.00 per share nor did it specify any reasons in connection therewith

event_claims.description: CSC/Pamplona confirmed willingness to increase its all-cash purchase price to $21.25 per share as a last and best offer if Mac-Gray agreed to two weeks of exclusivity.
event_claims.quote_text: Later that same day, the representative of CSC/Pamplona called the members of the Transaction Committee to confirm that CSC/Pamplona were willing to increase their all-cash purchase price to $21.25 per share as a last and best offer, provided that Mac-Gray agree to a two week period of exclusive negotiations

event_claims.description: Mac-Gray authorized entry into an exclusivity agreement providing exclusive negotiations until 5 p.m. ET on October 12, 2013.
event_claims.quote_text: the Special Committee authorized Mac-Gray to enter into an exclusivity agreement providing for exclusive negotiations until 5 p.m. ET on October 12, 2013 on the terms included in the CSC/Pamplona final proposal

event_claims.description: CSC, Pamplona and Mac-Gray executed the exclusivity agreement providing for exclusive negotiations until 5 p.m. ET on October 12, 2013.
event_claims.quote_text: On September 24, 2013, CSC, Pamplona and Mac-Gray executed the exclusivity agreement providing for exclusive negotiations until 5 p.m. ET on October 12, 2013

event_claims.description: Pamplona committed to fund 100% of the capital needed for closing, with no financing contingency.
event_claims.quote_text: the fact that there would be no financing contingency and that Pamplona was committing to fund 100% of the capital needed for closing

event_claims.description: It was determined that Moab would not rollover any Moab shares in the transaction.
event_claims.quote_text: As a result of such discussions, it was determined that Moab would not rollover any Moab shares in the transaction
